questions.question: D-Day (June 6, 1944) involved approximately how many troops and what were the five beach code names?
options: 156,000 troops; Omaha, Utah, Gold, Juno, Sword
80,000 troops; Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, Epsilon
250,000 troops; Red, Blue, Green, White, Black
156,000 troops; North, South, East, West, Center
questions.answer: 156,000 troops; Omaha, Utah, Gold, Juno, Sword
questions.explanation: D-Day (Operation Overlord) on June 6, 1944 involved approximately 156,000 Allied troops in the largest seaborne invasion in history. The five beaches were: Utah and Omaha (US), Gold and Sword (British), Juno (Canadian). General Eisenhower commanded.
questions.memory_hook: D-Day June 6, 1944: 156K troops, 5 beaches: Utah + Omaha (US), Gold + Sword (UK), Juno (Canada). Eisenhower commanded.

questions.question: What is a circadian rhythm and what body structure acts as the master clock?
options: ~24-hour internal biological clock; the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) in the hypothalamus
Weekly biological cycle; the pineal gland
Monthly cycle; the pituitary gland
Annual cycle; the cerebellum
questions.answer: ~24-hour internal biological clock; the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN) in the hypothalamus
questions.explanation: Circadian rhythms regulate sleep, hormones, and metabolism over ~24 hours. The SCN (suprachiasmatic nucleus) in the hypothalamus acts as the master clock. Light exposure resets it. Jet lag occurs when you rapidly cross time zones.
questions.memory_hook: Circadian = ~24-hour clock. Master clock = SCN in hypothalamus. Light resets it. Jet lag = disrupted rhythm.

questions.question: Which cyclist won the Tour de France 5 times, also won 5 Giro d'Italia titles, and is nicknamed 'The Cannibal'?
options: Eddy Merckx (Belgian)
Jacques Anquetil (French)
Bernard Hinault (French)
Miguel Indurain (Spanish)
questions.explanation: Eddy Merckx won the Tour de France 5 times (1969-72, 1974) and the Giro d'Italia 5 times — arguably the greatest cyclist of all time. He won 525 career races, earning the nickname 'The Cannibal.'
questions.memory_hook: Eddy Merckx: Belgian, 5 Tour de France + 5 Giro, 525 career wins. 'The Cannibal.'

questions.question: West Side Story (1957/1961) was inspired by Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet. Who wrote its music?
options: Leonard Bernstein (music) and Stephen Sondheim (lyrics)
George Gershwin
Cole Porter
Andrew Lloyd Webber
questions.explanation: West Side Story had music by Leonard Bernstein and lyrics by Stephen Sondheim. The 1961 film won 10 Oscars. The 2021 Spielberg remake won Best Supporting Actress (Ariana DeBose).
questions.memory_hook: West Side Story: Bernstein music + Sondheim lyrics. 1961 film = 10 Oscars. Spielberg's 2021 remake won Supporting Actress.

questions.question: Bikini Atoll in the Marshall Islands: why is it famous, and how did 'bikini' swimwear get its name?
options: US nuclear tests (1946); designer Réard named the explosive new swimsuit after the 'explosive' tests
It had beautiful beaches and the name was adopted for its beauty
A French film was made there in the 1940s
The bikini crab lives there
questions.answer: US nuclear tests (1946); designer Réard named the explosive new swimsuit after the 'explosive' tests
questions.explanation: The US conducted nuclear tests at Bikini Atoll starting July 1946. French designer Louis Réard unveiled his tiny two-piece swimsuit shortly after and named it 'bikini' to suggest its 'explosive' impact. The islanders were relocated and never fully returned.
questions.memory_hook: Bikini Atoll: US nuclear tests 1946. Swimsuit named 'bikini' for its 'explosive' impact. Islanders never returned.

questions.question: John Locke's idea that citizens have the right to revolt if government fails them directly influenced which document?
options: U.S. Declaration of Independence
U.S. Constitution
Magna Carta
French Declaration of the Rights of Man
questions.explanation: Jefferson borrowed Locke's ideas from 'Second Treatise of Government' (1689) directly. Locke's 'life, liberty, property' became 'life, liberty, pursuit of happiness' in Jefferson's hand.
questions.memory_hook: Locke → Declaration of Independence. 'Life, liberty, property' → 'life, liberty, pursuit of happiness.' Jefferson borrowed directly.

questions.question: What was 'The Rachel' and why is it famous in pop culture history?
options: Jennifer Aniston's Friends hairstyle — reportedly the most-copied hairstyle in American history
A cocktail invented on the set of 'Friends'
The nickname for Rachel Green's apartment on Friends
A fashion trend from the show's famous 'Rachel's closet' scenes
questions.answer: Jennifer Aniston's Friends hairstyle — reportedly the most-copied hairstyle in American history
questions.explanation: 'The Rachel' — Jennifer Aniston's layered blowout haircut in early Friends episodes — became the most-copied hairstyle in American history, according to her own hairdresser. Friends ran 1994-2004 and remains one of the most-watched streaming shows.
questions.memory_hook: The Rachel: Aniston's Friends haircut (1994-96), most copied hairstyle in US history per her own stylist.

questions.question: Alan Turing's work cracking Nazi Enigma codes during WWII is estimated to have saved how many lives?
options: An estimated 14 million lives
About 1 million lives
About 100,000 lives
The number is unknown and disputed
questions.explanation: Alan Turing's Bombe machine helped crack Enigma codes, shortening the war by an estimated 2 years and saving approximately 14 million lives. He was prosecuted for homosexuality in 1952 and died in 1954. He appeared on the British £50 note from 2021.
questions.memory_hook: Turing: Enigma codebreaker, est. 14M lives saved. Prosecuted for being gay. Now on £50 note.

questions.question: The Peloponnese in Greece is connected to the mainland by what narrow land bridge, and what famous canal runs through it?
options: The Isthmus of Corinth; the Corinth Canal (built 1893)
The Isthmus of Sparta; the Sparta Canal
The Isthmus of Athens; the Athens Canal
A land bridge near Thessaly
questions.answer: The Isthmus of Corinth; the Corinth Canal (built 1893)
questions.explanation: The Peloponnese connects to mainland Greece via the Isthmus of Corinth, through which the Corinth Canal was built in 1893. Major ancient cities (Sparta, Corinth, Olympia) are on the Peloponnese.
questions.memory_hook: Peloponnese: connected via Isthmus of Corinth. Corinth Canal cut through it in 1893. Sparta, Corinth, Olympia all there.

questions.question: Dorothea Lange's 'Migrant Mother' (1936): who was the woman in the photo?
options: Florence Owens Thompson, a Cherokee woman who was not paid for the photograph
Dorothea Lange herself
An anonymous farm worker whose identity was never discovered
A professional model hired by the Farm Security Administration
questions.answer: Florence Owens Thompson, a Cherokee woman who was not paid for the photograph
questions.explanation: 'Migrant Mother' shows Florence Owens Thompson, a 32-year-old Cherokee woman. Lange took 7 photographs; the famous one was the last. Thompson was never paid and was upset by the image's fame.
questions.memory_hook: Migrant Mother: Florence Owens Thompson, Cherokee, 32 years old. Lange took 7 shots; last one became famous. Thompson never paid.

questions.question: La La Land won 6 Oscars at the 2017 ceremony but is most famous for what chaos?
options: Warren Beatty read the wrong winner's name for Best Picture — Moonlight actually won
The director gave a tearful speech that lasted 45 minutes
All 6 awards were given to the wrong film
Emma Stone refused to accept her Oscar live
questions.answer: Warren Beatty read the wrong winner's name for Best Picture — Moonlight actually won
questions.explanation: In one of cinema's most dramatic moments, Warren Beatty and Faye Dunaway announced La La Land as Best Picture winner. La La Land producers were mid-speech when the mistake was caught and Moonlight announced as the real winner. La La Land did win 6 other Oscars.
questions.memory_hook: La La Land 2017: 6 Oscars but infamously had Best Picture read for it by mistake. Moonlight was the actual winner.

questions.question: In cricket, what is an 'over' and how many deliveries does it contain?
options: 6 legal deliveries from one end; bowlers alternate ends after each over
5 deliveries; batsmen switch ends after each over
8 deliveries; the same bowler can bowl multiple overs in a row
6 deliveries; the same bowler can bowl two consecutive overs
questions.answer: 6 legal deliveries from one end; bowlers alternate ends after each over
questions.explanation: An over = 6 legal deliveries from one end. A different bowler must bowl from the opposite end after each over. T20 = 20 overs per side; One Day International (ODI) = 50 overs per side.
questions.memory_hook: Over: 6 legal deliveries, then change ends and bowler. T20 = 20 overs/side. ODI = 50 overs/side.
